nodes.title: Alexandrov's Theorem
nodes.description: Any convex polyhedral metric on a sphere is realized by a unique convex polyhedron — so a valid gluing of a polygon folds to exactly one convex solid.

nodes.title: Amortized Analysis
nodes.description: Bound the average cost per operation over a worst-case sequence using aggregate, accounting, or potential methods.

nodes.title: Approximation Algorithms
nodes.description: For NP-hard optimization, find a provably near-optimal solution in polynomial time with a guaranteed ratio.

nodes.title: All-Pairs Shortest Paths via DP
nodes.description: Compute shortest paths between every pair by a DP over path length, like repeated matrix multiplication.

nodes.title: Asymptotic Notation (Big-O)
nodes.description: O, Omega, Theta describe how running time grows with input size, hiding constants and lower-order terms.

nodes.title: Asymptotics & Integral Bounds
nodes.description: Big-O/Theta/Omega compare growth rates; sums are bounded by integrals, and Stirling approximates n!.

nodes.title: AVL Trees (Balanced BSTs)
nodes.description: A self-balancing BST that keeps subtree heights within 1, guaranteeing O(log n) operations via rotations.

nodes.title: Bellman–Ford Algorithm
nodes.description: Relax all edges V-1 times to find shortest paths with negative weights, and detect negative cycles.

nodes.title: Big–Little–Big Lemma
nodes.description: At a flat-foldable vertex, the crease inside a strictly-smallest angle (a local minimum) is forced to differ in M/V from its two neighbors.

nodes.title: Binary Heaps & Priority Queues
nodes.description: An array-based near-complete tree with the heap property, giving O(log n) insert and extract-min/max.

nodes.title: Binary Search Trees
nodes.description: A tree keeping the BST property (left < node < right) supports ordered set operations in O(height).

nodes.title: Binomial Coefficients & Pascal's Triangle
nodes.description: C(n,k) counts k-subsets; the binomial theorem and Pascal's identity organize them.

nodes.title: Bipartite Matching & Hall's Theorem
nodes.description: A matching pairs up vertices with no shared endpoints; Hall's condition tells exactly when one side can be fully matched.

nodes.title: Box Pleating & Universal Hinge Patterns
nodes.description: Designing on a 45°/90° grid so a single fixed hinge pattern can fold any cube-complex (polycube) shape.

nodes.title: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
nodes.description: Explore a graph in layers from a source, computing shortest paths by edge count in O(V+E).

nodes.title: Carpenter's Rule Theorem
nodes.description: Any planar open chain or simple polygon can be straightened/convexified without self-intersection — 2D chains never lock.

nodes.title: Cauchy's Rigidity Theorem
nodes.description: A convex polyhedron is uniquely determined (up to congruence) by its faces and how they're glued — convex polyhedra are rigid.

nodes.title: Change of Basis
nodes.description: The same vector/map has different coordinates in different bases; related by B = M^{-1}AM.

nodes.title: Clustering
nodes.description: Partition points into groups by similarity; greedy and MST-based methods give approximation guarantees.

nodes.title: Cofactor Expansion
nodes.description: Compute det A by expanding along a row/column using signed minors (cofactors).

nodes.title: Column Space C(A)
nodes.description: All linear combinations of A's columns — the set of b for which Ax=b is solvable.

nodes.title: Comparison Sorting & Its Lower Bound
nodes.description: Algorithms that order items only by comparing pairs need Omega(n log n) comparisons in the worst case.

nodes.title: Competitive Analysis (Online Algorithms)
nodes.description: Judge an online algorithm (no future knowledge) by its worst-case ratio to the optimal offline solution.

nodes.title: Complete Solution of Ax = b
nodes.description: Every solution is x_particular + x_nullspace; solvable iff b∈C(A); the rank decides how many solutions.

nodes.title: Complex Matrices & the FFT
nodes.description: Hermitian/unitary generalize symmetric/orthogonal to ℂ; the Fourier matrix factors to give the O(n log n) FFT.

nodes.title: Complexity Classes: P, NP, EXP
nodes.description: Classify problems by the resources to solve or verify them; P is efficiently solvable, NP efficiently verifiable.

nodes.title: Computational Geometry
nodes.description: Algorithms for geometric objects: sweep lines for intersections, convex hull, and closest pair.

nodes.title: Computational Problems & Algorithms
nodes.description: A problem maps inputs to correct outputs; an algorithm is a procedure that solves every instance, proven correct by induction.

nodes.title: Conditional Probability & Bayes' Theorem
nodes.description: Conditioning updates probability given information; Bayes reverses the conditioning direction.

nodes.title: Configuration Space
nodes.description: The space of all valid placements of a linkage; folding motions are paths in it, and 'locked' means a disconnected component.

nodes.title: Counting: Sum, Product & Bijections
nodes.description: Count by sum/product rules, permutations and combinations, or by a bijection to a set you can already count.

nodes.title: Cramer's Rule & Volume
nodes.description: Solve Ax=b by ratios of determinants; det also measures volume scaling of the map A.

nodes.title: Crease Pattern
nodes.description: The flat diagram of all fold lines drawn on the unfolded sheet of paper.

nodes.title: D-Form
nodes.description: A smooth convex surface made by gluing the perimeters of two convex curves of equal length.

nodes.title: DAG Shortest Paths (Relaxation)
nodes.description: On a DAG, relaxing edges in topological order gives shortest paths in O(V+E), even with negative weights.

nodes.title: Set vs Sequence Interfaces
nodes.description: Separate the operations a data structure must support (interface) from how it does so (implementation).

nodes.title: Dehn Invariant
nodes.description: An algebraic quantity (from edge lengths and dihedral angles) that must match for two 3D polyhedra to be scissors-congruent.

nodes.title: Depth-First Search (DFS)
nodes.description: Explore as deep as possible before backtracking; classifies edges and exposes graph structure in O(V+E).

nodes.title: Derandomization
nodes.description: Convert a randomized algorithm into a deterministic one, e.g. by the method of conditional expectations.

nodes.title: Determinant
nodes.description: A single number det A that is 0 exactly when A is singular; equals signed volume and the product of pivots.

nodes.title: Diagonalization & Powers of A
nodes.description: If A has n independent eigenvectors, A = SΛS^{-1}, so A^k = SΛ^kS^{-1}.

nodes.title: Dijkstra's Algorithm
nodes.description: Greedily settle the nearest unsettled vertex using a priority queue; O(E + V log V) for nonnegative weights.

nodes.title: Divide-and-Conquer Recurrences & Master Theorem
nodes.description: Recurrences T(n)=aT(n/b)+f(n) are solved by recursion trees, summarized by the master theorem.

nodes.title: Divisibility, GCD & the Euclidean Algorithm
nodes.description: The gcd of two integers is computed by repeated remainders (Euclid), and is an integer combination of them (Bezout).

nodes.title: Dynamic Arrays
nodes.description: A resizable array that doubles capacity when full, giving O(1) amortized append with O(1) indexing.

nodes.title: Dynamic Programming (SRTBOT)
nodes.description: Solve a problem via overlapping subproblems related by a recurrence, evaluated once each in topological order.

nodes.title: Edge Unfolding
nodes.description: Unfolding where all cuts run along polyhedron edges; existence for all convex polyhedra is a famous open problem.

nodes.title: Eigenvalues & Eigenvectors
nodes.description: Directions x that A only scales: Ax=λx; found from det(A−λI)=0.

nodes.title: Expectation & Linearity
nodes.description: The expected value is the probability-weighted average; expectation is linear even for dependent variables.

nodes.title: FFT & Polynomial Multiplication
nodes.description: Multiply degree-n polynomials in O(n log n) by evaluating at roots of unity and interpolating back.

nodes.title: Fixed-Angle Linkage (Protein Folding)
nodes.description: A 3D linkage where both bar lengths and the angles between consecutive bars are fixed; models protein backbones.

nodes.title: NP-Hardness of Flat Foldability
nodes.description: Deciding global flat-foldability of a given crease pattern is NP-hard (Bern & Hayes 1996), driven by the layer-ordering constraint.

nodes.title: Flat-Foldability
nodes.description: Whether a crease pattern can be folded into a flat (zero-thickness) state without the paper crossing itself.

nodes.title: Floyd–Warshall Algorithm
nodes.description: All-pairs shortest paths in O(V^3) by a DP that allows intermediate vertices one at a time.

nodes.title: Fold-and-One-Cut
nodes.description: Fold a sheet flat so that a single straight cut produces any desired arrangement of polygons / line drawing.

nodes.title: The Four Fundamental Subspaces
nodes.description: Every matrix has C(A), N(A), C(A^T), N(A^T) with dimensions r, n−r, r, m−r — and they pair up as orthogonal complements.

nodes.title: Gaussian Elimination
nodes.description: Systematic row operations that reduce A to upper-triangular form, then solve by back-substitution.

nodes.title: General Unfolding (Source & Star)
nodes.description: Unfolding with cuts allowed across faces; convex polyhedra always have one via the source or star unfolding.

nodes.title: Generating Functions
nodes.description: Encode a sequence as the coefficients of a power series; algebra on the series solves counting and recurrences.

nodes.title: Gluing Tree
nodes.description: A combinatorial description of how a polygon's boundary is glued to itself; the search structure for finding Alexandrov gluings.

nodes.title: Gram–Schmidt Orthogonalization
nodes.description: Turn any independent set into an orthonormal one by subtracting projections, one vector at a time.

nodes.title: Graph Coloring
nodes.description: Assign colors to vertices so adjacent ones differ; the minimum number needed is the chromatic number.

nodes.title: Graph Representations
nodes.description: Store a graph as adjacency lists (sparse-friendly) or an adjacency matrix (dense, O(1) edge test).

nodes.title: Graphs: Walks, Paths & Connectivity
nodes.description: A graph is vertices joined by edges; walks, paths, cycles and connectivity describe how to move through it.

nodes.title: Greedy Algorithms & the Exchange Argument
nodes.description: Build a solution by locally optimal choices; prove optimality by an exchange argument or matroid structure.

nodes.title: Hash Tables (Chaining)
nodes.description: Store keys in buckets chosen by a hash function; with a good hash, search/insert/delete are O(1) expected.

nodes.title: Hinged Dissection
nodes.description: A chain of polygons hinged at vertices that can refold to form any of several target shapes; such universal dissections always exist.

nodes.title: HP Model of Protein Folding
nodes.description: A lattice model where amino acids are Hydrophobic or Polar; the optimal (max H–H contacts) folding is NP-complete.

nodes.title: Huffman Coding
nodes.description: Build an optimal prefix-free code greedily by repeatedly merging the two least-frequent symbols.

nodes.title: Pleat Folding & the Hyperbolic Paraboloid
nodes.description: The classic concentric-square 'hypar' model provably cannot fold without extra creases — straight creases stay straight and flat polygons stay flat.

nodes.title: Incidence Matrices (Graphs & Networks)
nodes.description: A matrix encoding a graph (edges×nodes); its four subspaces are loops, potentials, and Kirchhoff's laws.

nodes.title: Inclusion–Exclusion
nodes.description: Count a union by adding sets, subtracting pairwise overlaps, adding triples, and so on.

nodes.title: Independence, Basis, Dimension
nodes.description: Independent vectors have only the trivial combination giving 0; a basis is an independent spanning set; its size is the dimension.

nodes.title: Independence
nodes.description: Events are independent when one's occurrence does not change the other's probability; mutual is stronger than pairwise.

nodes.title: Mathematical Induction
nodes.description: Prove P(n) for all n by establishing a base case and that P(n) implies P(n+1).

nodes.title: Infinitesimal Rigidity
nodes.description: A linear-algebra test: a framework is infinitesimally rigid if the rigidity matrix admits only trivial velocity assignments.

nodes.title: Interval Scheduling
nodes.description: Select the most non-overlapping intervals by repeatedly taking the one that finishes earliest.

nodes.title: Invariants & State Machines
nodes.description: Model a process as states with transitions; a preserved invariant proves what the process can and cannot reach.

nodes.title: Johnson's Algorithm
nodes.description: All-pairs shortest paths on sparse graphs: reweight with Bellman-Ford to remove negatives, then run Dijkstra from each vertex.

nodes.title: Similar Matrices & Jordan Form
nodes.description: Similar matrices B=M^{-1}AM share eigenvalues; the Jordan form is the canonical near-diagonal form when A can't be diagonalized.

nodes.title: Kawasaki's Theorem
nodes.description: A single vertex of degree 2n folds flat iff its alternating angle sum is 0° — equivalently, odd-indexed angles sum to 180°.

nodes.title: Kempe's Universality Theorem
nodes.description: There is a linkage whose tracing joint draws any given polynomial (algebraic) curve — 'a linkage to sign your name'.

nodes.title: Kruskal's Algorithm
nodes.description: Sort all edges and add each lightest edge that joins two different components, using union-find.

nodes.title: Laman's Theorem
nodes.description: A graph is minimally generically rigid in 2D iff it has 2n−3 edges and every k-vertex subset spans at most 2k−3 edges.

nodes.title: Least Squares
nodes.description: Best-fit solution to an inconsistent Ax=b, found from the normal equations A^TA x̂ = A^Tb.

nodes.title: Linear Recurrences
nodes.description: Constant-coefficient recurrences solve via a characteristic equation plus a particular solution.

nodes.title: Linear-Time Sorting (Counting & Radix)
nodes.description: When keys are small integers, counting and radix sort order n items in O(n) by avoiding comparisons.

nodes.title: Three Pictures of Ax = b
nodes.description: A linear system has three views: rows (intersecting planes), columns (combination of column vectors), and the matrix form Ax=b.

nodes.title: Linear-Time Selection (Median of Medians)
nodes.description: Find the k-th smallest element in worst-case O(n) by choosing a provably good pivot via medians of groups.

nodes.title: Linear Transformations
nodes.description: Maps T with T(cu+dv)=cT(u)+dT(v); once bases are chosen, every such map IS a matrix.

nodes.title: Linkage
nodes.description: A collection of fixed-length bars joined at hinges (a graph with edge lengths); the basic object of linkage folding.

nodes.title: Locked Linkage
nodes.description: A linkage stuck in a configuration it can't continuously move out of (to straighten/convexify) without self-crossing.

nodes.title: LU Factorization
nodes.description: Elimination written as A = LU: lower-triangular L (multipliers) times upper-triangular U (pivots).

nodes.title: Maekawa's Theorem
nodes.description: At every interior vertex of a flat-foldable crease pattern,

nodes.title: Map Folding
nodes.description: Given an m×n grid of creases with M/V labels, decide if it folds flat into a unit stack — easy in 1D, open/hard in general 2D.

nodes.title: Markov Matrices
nodes.description: Non-negative columns summing to 1; λ=1 is always an eigenvalue and its eigenvector is the steady state.

nodes.title: Matrix Exponential & Differential Equations
nodes.description: Solve du/dt = Au with u(t)=e^{At}u(0); compute e^{At}=Se^{Λt}S^{-1}.

nodes.title: Matrix Inverse
nodes.description: A^{-1} undoes A (AA^{-1}=I); it exists iff A is square with full rank, found by Gauss–Jordan.

nodes.title: Matrix Multiplication
nodes.description: The product AB, understood four equivalent ways: entry dot-products, columns, rows, and a sum of rank-one pieces.

nodes.title: Merge Sort
nodes.description: Divide the array in half, sort each recursively, and merge the sorted halves in linear time — O(n log n).

nodes.title: Minimum Spanning Trees
nodes.description: The cheapest set of edges connecting all vertices; greedy works thanks to the cut property.

nodes.title: Modular Arithmetic
nodes.description: Arithmetic on remainders mod n; inverses exist when gcd with n is 1, and Fermat/Euler give fast exponentiation.

nodes.title: Mountain–Valley Assignment
nodes.description: A labeling of every crease as a mountain (folds away) or valley (folds toward you).

nodes.title: Network Flow & Max-Flow Min-Cut
nodes.description: Push the most flow from source to sink; the maximum flow equals the minimum cut capacity.

nodes.title: NP-Completeness & Reductions
nodes.description: A problem is NP-complete if it is in NP and every NP problem reduces to it; one polynomial solution would collapse P=NP.

nodes.title: Nullspace N(A)
nodes.description: All solutions to Ax=0 — a subspace of R^n whose dimension is n − rank.

nodes.title: Orthogonal Matrix
nodes.description: A square matrix Q with orthonormal columns: Q^TQ=I, so Q^{-1}=Q^T; preserves lengths and angles.

nodes.title: Orthogonality & Orthogonal Complements
nodes.description: Vectors are orthogonal when their dot product is 0; a subspace's orthogonal complement holds everything perpendicular to it.

nodes.title: Pigeonhole Principle
nodes.description: If n+1 items go into n boxes, some box holds two — a simple counting fact with surprising consequences.

nodes.title: Planar Graphs & Euler's Formula
nodes.description: A planar graph can be drawn without crossings; vertices, edges and faces satisfy v - e + f = 2.

nodes.title: Polyhedron Unfolding
nodes.description: Cutting a polyhedron's surface so it unfolds flat into one non-overlapping piece (a net).

nodes.title: Positive Definite Matrices
nodes.description: Symmetric A with x^TAx>0 for all x≠0; equivalently all eigenvalues, pivots, and leading minors are positive.

nodes.title: Prim's Algorithm
nodes.description: Grow an MST from one vertex, repeatedly adding the cheapest edge leaving the current tree via a priority queue.

nodes.title: Probability: Sample Spaces & the Four-Step Method
nodes.description: Model an experiment by a sample space and a probability on outcomes; the four-step method makes it systematic.

nodes.title: Projection onto a Subspace
nodes.description: The closest point in a subspace to b; given by the projection matrix P = A(A^TA)^{-1}A^T.

nodes.title: Proof Methods
nodes.description: Standard ways to prove a statement: direct, by cases, by contrapositive, and by contradiction.

nodes.title: Propositions, Predicates & Quantifiers
nodes.description: A proposition is a statement that is true or false; predicates add variables, and quantifiers (for-all, exists) bind them.

nodes.title: Pseudoinverse
nodes.description: A^+ = VΣ^+U^T extends the inverse to any matrix; gives least-squares / minimum-norm solutions.

nodes.title: Pseudopolynomial Time & Subset Sum
nodes.description: A DP whose time depends on a numeric value (not just input length) runs in pseudopolynomial time — e.g. subset sum and knapsack.

nodes.title: Pointed Pseudotriangulation
nodes.description: A planar subdivision into pseudotriangles (3 convex corners) with every vertex pointed; its mechanics drive the carpenter's-rule unfolding.

nodes.title: QR Factorization
nodes.description: A = QR with Q orthonormal and R upper-triangular; the stable way to do least squares.

nodes.title: Random Variables & Distributions
nodes.description: A random variable maps outcomes to numbers; its distribution (PMF) lists the probability of each value.

nodes.title: Random Walks & Gambler's Ruin
nodes.description: A walker takes random +/-1 steps; gambler's ruin computes the probability and duration before hitting a boundary.

nodes.title: Randomized Algorithms
nodes.description: Use random choices for speed or simplicity; Las Vegas is always correct, Monte Carlo may err with small probability.

nodes.title: Rank-One Matrices
nodes.description: The simplest nonzero matrices, A = uv^T; every rank-r matrix is a sum of r of them.

nodes.title: Rank
nodes.description: The number of pivots = dimension of the column space = number of independent rows/columns; it governs solvability.

nodes.title: Relations & Partial Orders
nodes.description: Binary relations classify by reflexivity/symmetry/transitivity; partial orders model dependency and enable scheduling.

nodes.title: Rigid Origami
nodes.description: Folding where faces stay rigid (flat) and only the creases move — the model for stiff materials, self-folding sheets, and architecture.

nodes.title: Rigidity (Generic & Minimal)
nodes.description: A framework is rigid if its only motions are trivial (rotations/translations); rigidity theory characterizes which graphs are rigid.

nodes.title: RSA Public-Key Cryptography
nodes.description: Public-key encryption built on modular exponentiation; security rests on the hardness of factoring n=pq.

nodes.title: Simple Fold
nodes.description: Folding all layers along one straight line by ±180°; the most restrictive, manufacturing-friendly fold model.

nodes.title: Single-Vertex Flat-Foldability
nodes.description: Deciding whether one interior vertex can fold flat — easy: check even degree, Kawasaki, Maekawa, and a layer-order pass.

nodes.title: Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
nodes.description: Any matrix factors as A = UΣV^T with orthogonal U,V and nonnegative diagonal Σ; the master factorization.

nodes.title: Solving Ax = 0: Pivots, Free Variables, RREF
nodes.description: Reduce A to RREF; pivot columns fix pivot variables, free columns give special solutions spanning the nullspace.

nodes.title: Strong Induction
nodes.description: An induction where the step may assume P holds for all values up to n, not just n itself.

nodes.title: Sublinear-Time Algorithms
nodes.description: Answer approximately by reading only a tiny sample of a huge input, in time less than its size.

nodes.title: Sums & Summation Techniques
nodes.description: Evaluate and bound finite sums via arithmetic/geometric formulas, perturbation, and telescoping.

nodes.title: Symmetric Matrices & the Spectral Theorem
nodes.description: A=A^T has real eigenvalues and orthonormal eigenvectors: A = QΛQ^T (spectral theorem).

nodes.title: Tensegrity
nodes.description: A framework with struts (can't shrink), cables (can't grow), and bars (fixed); stabilized by an equilibrium stress.

nodes.title: Topological Sort
nodes.description: Linearly order a DAG so every edge points forward; the prerequisite of DAG dynamic programming.

nodes.title: Transpose & Permutation Matrices
nodes.description: A^T swaps rows and columns; permutation matrices P reorder rows and handle pivoting (PA=LU).

nodes.title: Tree Method (Origami Design)
nodes.description: Design an origami base by packing the legs of a stick-figure tree as circles/rivers on the square, then filling regions with molecules.

nodes.title: Trees & Spanning Trees
nodes.description: A tree is a connected acyclic graph with n-1 edges; a spanning tree connects all vertices of a graph minimally.

nodes.title: Uniaxial Base
nodes.description: A folded base whose flaps all lie perpendicular to a single line (axis), so its shadow is exactly a tree.

nodes.title: Union-Find (Disjoint Sets)
nodes.description: Maintain disjoint sets with near-constant find/union via union-by-rank and path compression.

nodes.title: Universal & Perfect Hashing
nodes.description: Pick a hash function at random from a universal family so no adversary can force many collisions.

nodes.title: Universal Molecule
nodes.description: An algorithm that fills a convex polygon (from the tree-method packing) with creases that fold it into the required uniaxial flaps.

nodes.title: van Emde Boas Trees
nodes.description: A recursive structure over a universe of size u giving predecessor/successor in O(log log u).

nodes.title: Variance & Deviation Bounds
nodes.description: Variance measures spread; Markov and Chebyshev bound the chance a variable strays far from its mean.

nodes.title: Vector Space & Subspace
nodes.description: A set closed under addition and scalar multiplication; a subspace is such a set living inside another (must contain 0).

nodes.title: Vertex Unfolding
nodes.description: A relaxed unfolding where pieces may stay connected only at single vertices (like a hinged chain of faces).

nodes.title: Weighted Shortest Paths: Overview
nodes.description: Find minimum-weight paths from a source; the right algorithm depends on edge signs and graph shape.

nodes.title: Well-Ordering Principle
nodes.description: Every nonempty set of nonnegative integers has a least element — a proof tool equivalent to induction.

nodes.title: The Word-RAM Model
nodes.description: The cost model: memory is words of w bits, and basic operations on a word take O(1) time.

nodes.title: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
nodes.description: Topic map of 6.046J concepts — EN with KO pairs.

nodes.title: Algorithms
nodes.description: Topic map of 6.006 concepts — EN with KO pairs.

nodes.title: Geometric Fabrication
nodes.description: Cross-course study map for fabrication — folding theory (6.849) + the linear algebra (18.06) and algorithmic foundations (6.006/6.046J/6.042J) it runs on.

nodes.title: Geometric Folding
nodes.description: Topic map of 6.849 concepts — EN with KO pairs.

nodes.title: Linear Algebra
nodes.description: Topic map of 18.06 concepts — EN with KO pairs.

nodes.title: Mathematics for Computer Science
nodes.description: Topic map of 6.042J concepts — EN with KO pairs.
